title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You hear a woman talking to a colleague about lunch.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Are we still going out for lunch today? I brought a sandwich, but I don't really want it. || Man: I was going to suggest the burger place, but it gets busy on Fridays. || Woman: The salad bar is quicker. || Man: True, but [8]let's go to the café inside the bookshop, because it is quiet and we can talk about the project there[/8]. We only need something simple. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Are we still going out for lunch today? I brought a sandwich, but I don't really want it.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I was going to suggest the burger place, but it gets busy on Fridays.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: True, but let's go to the café inside the bookshop, because it is quiet and we can talk about the project there. We only need something simple. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

questions.question: Where do they decide to have lunch?
questions.explanation: They decide to go to the café inside the bookshop because it is quiet and they can discuss the project.

extracts.context: You hear two people talking about a town bus service.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Have you seen the new bus times? My morning bus now leaves five minutes earlier. || Woman: That might be annoying, but the evening buses run later than before. || Man: That helps people coming back from the cinema. || Woman: Yes, although [9]I think the best change is the new route past the hospital, because lots of older people asked for that[/9]. The ticket price is the same. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

dialogue.text: Have you seen the new bus times? My morning bus now leaves five minutes earlier.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That might be annoying, but the evening buses run later than before.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That helps people coming back from the cinema.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, although I think the best change is the new route past the hospital, because lots of older people asked for that. The ticket price is the same. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

questions.question: What change does the woman like best?
questions.explanation: The woman says the best change is the new route past the hospital.

extracts.context: You hear a girl leaving a message for her friend.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker: Hi, Jess. I'm calling about the tennis match tomorrow. I know we planned to meet at the courts at nine, but my dad can only drive me after breakfast. || Speaker: [10]Could we meet at ten instead, and practise for half an hour before the match starts[/10]? I have already told Lily, and she says that's fine. Please text me tonight. Please check the information carefully and speak to a member of staff if anything is still unclear, especially if your plans have changed today.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: Hi, Jess. I'm calling about the tennis match tomorrow. I know we planned to meet at the courts at nine, but my dad can only drive me after breakfast.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: Could we meet at ten instead, and practise for half an hour before the match starts? I have already told Lily, and she says that's fine. Please text me tonight. Please check the information carefully and speak to a member of staff if anything is still unclear, especially if your plans have changed today.

questions.question: What does the girl want to change?
questions.explanation: She asks if they can meet at ten instead of nine, so she wants to change the time.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new jacket.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: That's a smart jacket. Did you buy it for your interview? || Man: Yes. I tried a black one first, but it looked too formal. The grey one was cheaper, but the sleeves were too short. || Woman: So this is the blue one? || Man: Yes. [11]It cost more than I planned, but it fitted properly and I can wear it for other things too[/11]. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

dialogue.text: That's a smart jacket. Did you buy it for your interview?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes. I tried a black one first, but it looked too formal. The grey one was cheaper, but the sleeves were too short.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: So this is the blue one?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes. It cost more than I planned, but it fitted properly and I can wear it for other things too. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

questions.question: Why did the man choose the blue jacket?
questions.explanation: The man says the blue jacket fitted properly, unlike the grey one with short sleeves.

extracts.context: You hear a teacher talking about a class project.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker: Before you leave, remember that your geography project is due next Tuesday. Some of you asked if you could work in groups of four, but groups of three are easier to manage. || Speaker: [12]Each group must choose one local problem, such as traffic or litter, and explain one possible solution[/12]. You do not need to make a video, but you should include at least two photographs. Please check the information carefully and speak to a member of staff if anything is still unclear, especially if your plans have changed today.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: Before you leave, remember that your geography project is due next Tuesday. Some of you asked if you could work in groups of four, but groups of three are easier to manage.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: Each group must choose one local problem, such as traffic or litter, and explain one possible solution. You do not need to make a video, but you should include at least two photographs. Please check the information carefully and speak to a member of staff if anything is still unclear, especially if your plans have changed today.

questions.question: What must students do for the project?

options.B: suggest a solution to a local problem

questions.explanation: The teacher says each group must choose a local problem and explain one possible solution.

extracts.context: You hear a woman talking about visiting a museum.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker: I visited the science museum with my nephew last weekend. The café was closed, so we had to eat sandwiches outside. Some rooms were crowded, especially the space section. || Speaker: [13]The best part for him was the area where children could press buttons and try simple experiments[/13]. I enjoyed the old photographs, but he stayed longest with the machines. Please check the information carefully and speak to a member of staff if anything is still unclear, especially if your plans have changed today.

dialogue.text: The best part for him was the area where children could press buttons and try simple experiments. I enjoyed the old photographs, but he stayed longest with the machines. Please check the information carefully and speak to a member of staff if anything is still unclear, especially if your plans have changed today.

questions.question: What did the woman's nephew enjoy most?
questions.explanation: She says the best part for her nephew was the area where children could press buttons and try simple experiments.
